subject: Chemistry
title: Oganesson: The Newest Noble Gas
description: A micro-lesson that makes Oganesson: The Newest Noble Gas usable.

hook.text: Element 118 sits at the bottom-right corner of the periodic table. Only a few atoms have ever existed.

buildup.text: Russian and American scientists created it in 2002 by smashing calcium atoms into californium targets.

discovery.text: They named it oganesson after Yuri Oganessian, a living scientist—an extremely rare honor.

twist.text: It's a noble gas on paper, but it probably acts nothing like one. Predictions say it behaves like a solid.

climax.text: It decays in less than a millisecond. We know it exists only from the debris it leaves behind.

punchline.text: The heaviest element. Gone in a millisecond. Noble in name only.

quiz.question: What is unusual about oganesson compared to other noble gases?
options: It may behave like a solid rather than a gas
It is the lightest noble gas
It occurs naturally in volcanic vents
